title: helm-ai-kernel chart values
description: Configuration schema for the helm-ai-kernel Helm chart. Documents the shape of every supported value, enables IDE autocomplete, and lets `helm install` reject malformed values before reaching the cluster.

replicaCount.description: Number of kernel pods to run. The kernel is stateless apart from its SQLite-backed receipts and ProofGraph store; horizontal scaling requires switching helm.storage.type to postgres.

image.description: Container image used by the kernel Deployment.

repository.description: Container image repository, without tag. Defaults to the public OCI image ghcr.io/mindburn-labs/helm-ai-kernel.

pullPolicy.description: Kubernetes image pull policy. Use IfNotPresent for pinned tags and Always when consuming the floating :latest tag.

tag.description: Overrides the image tag. Leave empty to inherit the chart's appVersion. Set explicitly when pinning to a digest-resolved release or to a development build.

imagePullSecrets.description: References to Kubernetes Secrets used to pull chart-managed images from private registries. Applied to the kernel Deployment and optional launchpad app Pods/Jobs/test Pod. Each entry must be a {name: <secret>} object.

name.description: Name of the Secret of type kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson in the release namespace.

nameOverride.description: Override the chart name component used to build resource names. Leave empty to use the chart name verbatim.

fullnameOverride.description: Override the full resource name prefix. When set, both the release name and chart name are ignored in resource naming.

serviceAccount.description: Kubernetes ServiceAccount used by the kernel pod.

create.description: Create a ServiceAccount for the kernel. Disable if you provide an externally managed one via .name.

annotations.description: Annotations attached to the ServiceAccount, for example IRSA / Workload Identity bindings.

name.description: Name of the ServiceAccount to use. If empty and create=true, a name is derived from the release fullname template.

podAnnotations.description: Annotations applied to the kernel Pod. Useful for Prometheus scrape annotations, Vault Agent Injector, eBPF instrumentation, etc.

podLabels.description: Extra labels applied to the kernel Pod in addition to the chart's standard selector labels.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level securityContext. Defaults run the kernel as non-root (UID/GID 65534, nobody) with fsGroup so the data volume is writable.

securityContext.description: Container-level securityContext. Defaults drop ALL Linux capabilities, disable privilege escalation, and mount the root filesystem read-only; helm.dataDir is the only writable path.

service.description: Kubernetes Service that exposes the kernel HTTP ports.

type.description: Kubernetes Service type. ClusterIP is recommended for production; place an Ingress or service-mesh gateway in front for external traffic.

port.description: Service port that fronts the kernel HTTP API container port.

healthPort.description: Service port that fronts the dedicated liveness/readiness HTTP endpoint container port.

metricsPort.description: Service port reserved for the Prometheus handler. Bound only when helm.metrics.enabled=true; see the metrics block for status.

ingress.description: Optional Kubernetes Ingress in front of the kernel Service. Disabled by default; production deployments typically use a service-mesh gateway or external load balancer.

className.description: IngressClass name (e.g. nginx, traefik, istio). Leave empty to use the cluster's default IngressClass.

annotations.description: Ingress annotations. Typical entries: cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer, nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/* options, alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/* options.

hosts.description: Hostnames and path mappings exposed by the Ingress.

host.description: Fully qualified hostname routed to the kernel Service.

paths.description: Paths under the host that should reach the kernel.

path.description: HTTP path prefix or exact match.

tls.description: TLS configuration for the Ingress. Each entry binds a Secret containing a certificate to one or more hostnames.

secretName.description: Name of the kubernetes.io/tls Secret holding the certificate and key.

hosts.description: Hostnames the certificate is valid for.

resources.description: Pod resource requests and limits. Defaults aim at a small production footprint; raise both for receipt-heavy workloads or when running the postgres storage backend in-cluster.

limits.description: Hard upper bounds enforced by the kubelet. Exceeding cpu throttles, exceeding memory OOM-kills the container.

cpu.description: CPU limit expressed in cores (e.g. 0.5) or millicores (e.g. 500m).

requests.description: Guaranteed resources used by the Kubernetes scheduler to place the pod.

cpu.description: CPU request in cores or millicores.

helm.description: HELM-kernel-specific configuration. This key is retained for one compatibility window while the chart identity and rendered resource names move to helm-ai-kernel.

bindAddr.description: Bind address used inside the pod. Kubernetes Services require 0.0.0.0; keep loopback only for sidecar topologies.

dataDir.description: Durable runtime directory for SQLite state, receipts, artifacts, and root keys. Matches the persistence volume mount path.

production.description: Production mode refuses to auto-generate a root signing key at startup and forces auth/key material to be supplied explicitly. Enable for any non-demo deployment.

proxy.description: Outbound proxy that forwards LLM traffic to an upstream provider. Disable when running fully air-gapped against an on-cluster model.

enabled.description: Enable the egress proxy that forwards governed LLM calls to the configured upstream.

upstream.description: Upstream LLM provider endpoint, including scheme and version path (e.g. https://api.openai.com/v1).

region.description: Region identifier emitted into receipts and used for data-residency policy decisions.

demoMode.description: Demo mode disables real connectors and uses ephemeral signing keys. Useful for local trials; must be false in production.

auth.description: Runtime API authentication. Production rendering refuses to omit these keys because protected evidence, receipt, tenant, and service-internal routes fail closed without key material.

adminAPIKey.description: Admin API key written into the generated auth Secret. Used for receipts/evidence administration endpoints. Leave empty when supplying an existingSecret.

serviceAPIKey.description: Service API key for machine-to-machine integrations. Leave empty when supplying an existingSecret.

existingSecret.description: Name of an existing Secret containing the admin and service API keys. Takes precedence over inline values.

adminAPIKeySecretKey.description: Key inside existingSecret that contains the admin API key.

serviceAPIKeySecretKey.description: Key inside existingSecret that contains the service API key.

tenantID.description: Server-bound tenant ID for tenant-scoped runtime routes. Request tenant headers or tenant_id query values must match this value and cannot select another tenant.

principalID.description: Server-bound principal ID for tenant-scoped runtime routes. X-Helm-Principal-ID may be supplied only when it matches this configured principal.

workspaceID.description: Server-bound workspace ID used by governed evaluation when the scoped emergency-stop fence is enabled. The request body never selects this value.

emergencyStop.description: Opt-in internal scoped dispatch fence. It is not an in-flight cancellation or complete operator Emergency Stop.

enabled.description: Enable signed internal FENCE commands and fail-closed scoped Guardian evaluation.

audience.description: Deployment-specific signed command audience. Required when enabled.

commandPublicKeys.description: Comma-separated key_id=hex-ed25519-public-key command verification keyring. Required when enabled unless existingSecret is supplied.

existingSecret.description: Existing Secret containing the command verification keyring.

commandPublicKeysSecretKey.description: Key inside existingSecret that contains the command verification keyring.

signing.description: Cryptographic signing key for ProofGraph receipts and evidence packs.

algorithm.description: Signature scheme. ed25519 is the default; ml-dsa-65 is the post-quantum option for environments that require NIST PQC.

key.description: Ed25519 root key seed as exactly 64 lowercase hex characters. Used only when existingSecret is empty; leave blank for production and inject via existingSecret instead.

existingSecret.description: Name of an existing Secret with a key named 'signing-key' containing the 64-char hex seed.

storage.description: Persistence backend for receipts, ProofGraph entries, and audit state.

type.description: Storage backend. sqlite is single-pod-only and best for development or trial deployments; postgres is required for replicaCount>1 and any HA topology.

path.description: Filesystem path for the SQLite database file. Must live under helm.dataDir so it survives pod restarts on the persistent volume.

password.description: Database password. Prefer existingSecret over inline values in production.

sslMode.description: libpq sslmode. Production postgres storage requires require, verify-ca, or verify-full; use verify-full against managed PostgreSQL services that ship a CA bundle.

maxOpenConns.description: Maximum open PostgreSQL connections. Zero leaves database/sql unlimited; production should set an explicit ceiling.

maxIdleConns.description: Maximum idle PostgreSQL connections retained by the pool.

connMaxLifetime.description: Maximum PostgreSQL connection lifetime as a Go duration string.

existingSecret.description: Existing Secret containing a DATABASE_URL entry; overrides inline host/port/database/user/password fields.

existingSecretKey.description: Key inside existingSecret that contains the database URL.

enabled.description: Enable the kernel Prometheus /metrics handler, metrics container port, and metrics Service entry.

serviceMonitor.description: Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitor rendered when metrics.enabled=true.

enabled.description: Render a ServiceMonitor object for prometheus-operator discovery.

labels.description: Extra labels for the ServiceMonitor (e.g. team, prometheus instance selector labels).

limits.description: Runtime limiter gates for global rate, actor/resource rate, process concurrency, and low-priority shedding.

policy.description: Policy reconciliation. The kernel verifies, compiles, and atomically swaps an EffectivePolicySnapshot at runtime; Kubernetes delivery is a policy-source backend, not runtime truth.

source.description: Where the runtime reconciler pulls policy bundles from.

kind.description: Policy source backend. controlplane is recommended for production; crd suits Kubernetes-native self-hosting; mountedFile is for OSS/local/bootstrap/demo use.

pollInterval.description: Runtime reconciler polling interval. Lost reload hints are recovered by polling, so do not raise this above 60s for production.

initialReconcileTimeout.description: How long the reconciler waits for the first valid snapshot before failing closed at startup.

url.description: Control-plane URL serving the policy bundle (HTTPS only in production).

auth.description: Authentication mode against the control plane.

mode.description: serviceAccountJWT (recommended) projects the pod's Kubernetes ServiceAccount token; bearerToken uses a static token from existingSecret.

bearerToken.description: Static bearer token. Prefer existingSecret over inline values; required only when mode=bearerToken.

existingSecret.description: Existing Secret containing the bearer token.

existingSecretKey.description: Key inside existingSecret holding the bearer token.

install.description: Render the HelmPolicyBundle CRD and RBAC. Set false when the cluster operator installs the CRD out-of-band.

allowedConfigMapNames.description: ConfigMap names the CRD policy reader may dereference. Empty renders no ConfigMap read grant.

allowedSecretNames.description: Secret names the CRD policy reader may dereference. Empty renders no Secret read grant.

mountedFile.description: Used when kind=mountedFile. ConfigMap or Secret bytes are a delivery backend; runtime reconciliation still verifies hash, signature, and provenance.

existingConfigMap.description: Name of an existing ConfigMap containing the policy bundle.

existingSecret.description: Name of an existing Secret containing the policy bundle. Use when policy material contains references that warrant Secret-grade protection.

mountPath.description: Filesystem path the policy ConfigMap/Secret is mounted at inside the pod.

failClosed.description: Behavior when a snapshot is missing, stale, or invalid.

requireInitialSnapshot.description: Refuse to serve traffic until the first valid policy snapshot is loaded.

onMissingSnapshot.description: Action when no snapshot is available. Keep on deny for production; allow is for emergency degraded-mode operation only.

onInvalidUpdate.description: Action when an updated bundle fails verification. keepLastKnownGood is the safe default; switch to deny for ultra-strict environments.

lastKnownGoodMaxAge.description: Maximum age of the last-known-good snapshot before the reconciler escalates to onMissingSnapshot behavior.

reloadHints.description: Out-of-band wake hints that trigger an immediate reconcile. The reconciler still polls — hints only reduce mean reconcile latency.

enabled.description: Accept wake hints. Disable to make polling the only reload trigger.

httpWakeEndpoint.description: Wake-only internal endpoint exposed for sidecars and operators (default /internal/policy/reconcile).

fsnotify.description: Watch the mounted-file policy path with inotify. Useful for development; in Kubernetes ConfigMap atomic-symlink swaps may bypass this.

k8sWatch.description: Watch the source ConfigMap/Secret/CRD via the Kubernetes API. Requires extra RBAC.

configReloaderSidecar.description: Optional configmap-reload sidecar that hits httpWakeEndpoint on mounted-file changes.

required.description: Reject unsigned policy heads during reconciliation. Production control-plane deployments must set this to true.

publicKey.description: Ed25519 public key (hex) used to verify canonical policy bundle bytes. Public keys are not secret, but existingSecret keeps values files free of key material.

existingSecret.description: Existing Secret containing the public key.

existingSecretKey.description: Key inside existingSecret holding the public key bytes.

configMap.description: Legacy alias for policy.source.mountedFile.existingConfigMap. Retained for one compatibility window.

fileName.description: Filename used when mounting the policy bundle into the pod (default serve-policy.toml).

name.description: Logical policy bundle name advertised in receipts.

profile.description: Runtime profile applied during policy compilation (default kubernetes).

referencePack.description: Path to the reference pack used to derive default-deny fallback rules at startup.

referencePackFileName.description: Filename of the reference pack inside the chart artifacts.

referencePackID.description: Stable identifier for the reference pack, emitted into receipts for downstream verification.

referencePackLabel.description: Human-readable label for the reference pack.

persistence.description: Persistent storage for helm.dataDir. Required when storage.type=sqlite or when receipts and evidence packs need to survive pod restarts.

enabled.description: Create a PersistentVolumeClaim for the data directory.

storageClass.description: StorageClass name. Leave empty to use the cluster default.

accessModes.description: PVC access modes. Use ReadWriteOnce for single-pod deployments; ReadWriteMany is only meaningful when also setting storage.type=postgres for the actual hot path.

size.description: PVC size in Kubernetes resource notation (e.g. 1Gi, 10Gi). Receipt-heavy workloads should provision generously.

existingClaim.description: Use an existing PVC instead of letting the chart create one. Useful when restoring from a snapshot or sharing a volume across releases.

probes.description: Liveness and readiness probes. Defaults target the dedicated health port and tolerate a slow first reconcile.

liveness.description: Pod liveness probe. Failure restarts the container.

readiness.description: Pod readiness probe. Failure removes the pod from the Service backend pool.

nodeSelector.description: Node selector constraint for the kernel pod.

tolerations.description: Pod tolerations applied to allow scheduling on tainted nodes.

items.description: Single toleration entry; mirrors the Kubernetes Toleration object.

affinity.description: Pod affinity/anti-affinity rules. Useful to spread across zones or co-locate with control-plane components.

postgresql.description: Optional bitnami/postgresql subchart values, enabled only when the dependency is declared and helm.storage.type=postgres.

enabled.description: Enable the bundled PostgreSQL subchart. Production deployments typically point to an externally managed PostgreSQL instead.

auth.description: PostgreSQL auth settings passed through to the subchart.

database.description: Database name created by the subchart.

username.description: Database user created by the subchart.

password.description: Database password. Prefer existingSecret over inline values.

existingSecret.description: Existing Secret containing the PostgreSQL password.

openrouter.description: OpenRouter credentials shared by launchpad apps (openclaw, hermes). The chart only accepts a Secret reference, never a raw API key in values.yaml.

apiKeySecretRef.description: Reference to a Secret in the release namespace that holds the OpenRouter API key.

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the OpenRouter API key.

key.description: Key inside the Secret whose value is the raw OpenRouter API key string.

launchpadApps.description: Launchpad apps deployed alongside the kernel inside the same helm release. Not a launchpad runtime path — kernel does not launch them via k8s API; these are pinned chart-managed Pods that mirror the local-container runbook for openclaw and hermes on a Kubernetes substrate.

nodeSelector.description: Optional nodeSelector applied to every launchpad-app Pod. Launchpad app images are published as linux/amd64 + linux/arm64 manifests; set this map explicitly only when architecture or node pool pinning is required.

networkPolicy.description: NetworkPolicy default-deny applied to launchpad-app Pods.

enabled.description: Create the NetworkPolicy object. Enforcement requires a CNI that honors NetworkPolicy (Calico, Cilium). On default minikube/kindnet the object is recorded but not enforced.

allowDNS.description: Allow egress to kube-system/kube-dns on UDP/TCP 53 so the egress sidecar can resolve openrouter.ai. Disable only in environments with NodeLocalDNSCache or other DNS topologies.

test.description: Connectivity test Pod (helm.sh/hook: test) used by `helm test`.

image.description: Image with a POSIX shell and wget for the test Pod. Pinned to busybox by default.

launchpadAppImage.description: Immutable image reference pinned by sha256 digest. Tags are not accepted — supply chain integrity is enforced by the chart helper.

digest.description: sha256 image digest including the `sha256:` prefix.

launchpadAppEgressSidecar.description: Egress proxy sidecar that fronts the workload's HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY traffic and enforces an allowlist on outbound CONNECT tunnels.

port.description: TCP port the sidecar binds inside the Pod's shared network namespace. The workload container reaches it via localhost:<port>.

allowlist.description: List of URL prefixes the sidecar is allowed to proxy. Anything else is denied at the sidecar level.

resources.description: Kubernetes resources (requests/limits) for the sidecar container.

launchpadAppDeployment.description: Long-running launchpad app deployed as a Kubernetes Deployment.

enabled.description: Render the Deployment, Service if any, and supporting resources for this app.

gatewayPort.description: TCP port the workload's gateway binds inside the container. With `--bind loopback` this is reachable only via `kubectl exec`, never via a Service.

tmpfsSize.description: Size limit for the tmpfs-backed emptyDir mounted at /tmp.

appStateSize.description: Size limit for the app_state emptyDir mounted at the workload's writable state directory.

resources.description: Kubernetes resources for the workload container.

readinessProbe.description: Tunables for the exec-based readiness probe. The exec command itself is hard-coded to helm-launchpad-openrouter-check inside the chart template.

launchpadAppJob.description: Hermes launchpad app deployed either as the proven short-lived smoke Job or as a long-lived gateway Deployment.

enabled.description: Render the selected Hermes workload mode and supporting resources.

mode.description: Hermes workload shape. `job` runs the single-query smoke path; `deployment` runs the long-lived Hermes gateway process.

provider.description: Provider passed to the default Hermes Job command.

model.description: Model passed to the default Hermes Job command.

query.description: Single query passed to the default Hermes Job command.

commandOverride.description: Full command array replacement. Leave empty to use the chart-managed command for the selected Hermes mode.

backoffLimit.description: Job backoffLimit. Zero means a single attempt — failure surfaces immediately.

activeDeadlineSeconds.description: Hard wall-clock limit for the Job; after this the Job is marked failed regardless of state.

ttlSecondsAfterFinished.description: Delete the Job (and its Pod) this many seconds after it reaches a terminal state, leaving evidence available to smoke audits for the window.

tmpfsSize.description: Size limit for the tmpfs-backed emptyDir mounted at /tmp.

appStateSize.description: Size limit for the app_state emptyDir mounted at the workload's writable state directory.

resources.description: Kubernetes resources for the workload container.
